rules.name: block-rm
rules.reason: Use recoverable deletion (`trash` or `gio trash`) instead of `rm`.

rules.name: block-find-delete
rules.reason: Destructive deletion is blocked. Use recoverable deletion workflows.

rules.name: block-git-push
rules.reason: Push overwrites remote. Commit locally, push your branch, and use 'gh pr create' to initiate review.

rules.name: block-git-checkout
rules.reason: Use "git worktree" for branches. For file restoration, maintain an audit trail: commit current work, diff to identify the rollback point, and apply the reverse diff to a new commit.

rules.name: block-git-switch
rules.reason: Branch switching in the main tree risks work loss. Use 'git worktree add <path> -b <branch>' to work in isolation.

rules.name: block-git-branch
rules.reason: Branch management should be PR-driven. Create new branches for feature work via GitHub/CLI.

rules.name: block-git-add-all
rules.reason: Blanket staging hides intent and risks committing secrets. Use targeted 'git add' for logical changes.

rules.name: block-git-add-interactive
rules.reason: Interactive staging is blocked. Review changes with 'git status' and 'git diff' before adding.

rules.name: block-git-stash
rules.reason: Stashing creates hidden state. Use frequent 'checkpoint' commits to save work-in-progress visibly.

rules.name: block-git-stash-pop
rules.reason: Stashing creates hidden state. Use frequent 'checkpoint' commits to save work-in-progress visibly.

rules.name: block-git-revert
rules.reason: Maintaining an audit trail is required. To roll back, commit your current work, diff the commits to identify the rollback point, and apply the reverse diff to a new commit.

rules.name: block-git-reset
rules.reason: Maintaining an audit trail is required. To roll back, commit your current work, diff the commits to identify the rollback point, and apply the reverse diff to a new commit.

rules.name: block-git-clean
rules.reason: Maintaining an audit trail is required. To roll back, commit your current work, diff the commits to identify the rollback point, and apply the reverse diff to a new commit.

rules.name: block-git-restore
rules.reason: Maintaining an audit trail is required. To roll back, commit your current work, diff the commits to identify the rollback point, and apply the reverse diff to a new commit.

rules.name: block-git-rebase
rules.reason: History rewrites can disrupt collaborators. Use the GitHub PR interface to handle merges and rebases.

rules.name: block-git-merge
rules.reason: History rewrites can disrupt collaborators. Use the GitHub PR interface to handle merges and rebases.

rules.name: block-git-commit-no-verify
rules.reason: Skipping hooks is blocked.

rules.name: block-git-commit-no-gpg-sign
rules.reason: Bypassing signing is blocked.

rules.name: block-bun-test
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-npm-test
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-pytest
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-vitest
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-python-pytest
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-uv-pytest
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-uv-vitest
rules.reason: All tests must be routed through a justfile recipe which properly initializes the testing env.

rules.name: block-direnv-tests
rules.reason: direnv exec bypasses justfile env initialization. Use 'just test' instead.
